name: Fiery Pork & Winter Bamboo Shoots Over Snow
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse bamboo shoots and slice into matchstick-sized pieces.
2. Slice pork to resemble the size of bamboo shoots and marinate with thin soy sauce and cornstarch for 10 minutes.
3. Peel garlic and slice thinly.
4. Combine chicken stock, chili paste, and sugar in a bowl.
5. Heat vegetable oil in a hot wok until very hot. Test by frying rice sticks; if it browns too quickly, reduce heat slightly.
6. Fry rice stick noodles in small batches until puffy white. Spread out on serving platter and keep warm.
7. Clean the wok and return it to a very hot state. Add peanut oil until it just begins to smoke.
8. Add drained pork and stir-fry briskly for about 2 minutes.
9. Add bamboo, garlic, and ginger. Toss with pork for 1 minute.
10. Return liquids to the wok and cover. Simmer for 3 minutes.
11. Remove lid and increase heat briefly to reduce sauce and add vinegar.
12. Ladle over rice stick noodles and serve.
